content.text: Research settled the design: R2 lifecycle rules are wrong here because Cloudflare uses them to physically delete objects. The safe solution is Worker-enforced logical expiry stored in `customMetadata.expiresAt`; legacy objects stay readable, new uploads default to `14d`, callers may override with `X-Static-Expires-In` (for example `2h`, `30d`, or `never`), and expired reads return `410 EXPIRED` while the R2 object remains intact. Expiring responses will be `no-store` so caches cannot outlive the policy.

Implementation is complete and release-ready. New uploads default to 14 days, support overrides such as `--expires 30d` or `--expires never`, return `410 EXPIRED` after expiry, and remain stored in R2. Legacy objects remain readable.

Cloudflare’s lifecycle rules were rejected because they physically delete objects. The implementation instead stores `expiresAt` in R2 custom metadata and enforces it through the Worker with `Cache-Control: no-store`. [R2 lifecycle rules](https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/buckets/object-lifecycles/), [R2 Workers API](https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/api/workers/workers-api-reference/), [Workers cache configuration](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/cache/configuration/)

- `nub run verify`: lint, formatting, TypeScript, 22 tests, Wrangler types, and production build passed.
- `nub audit --audit-level=high`: no known vulnerabilities.
- Leenk commit: `418e02b`
- Managed client commit: `a3e2bdf`
- Unrelated `.node-version` and dotfiles `AGENTS.md` changes remain untouched.

content.text:
Expiration support is live in production on `yusoofsh.id`.

- New uploads default to exactly 14 days.
- Overrides support `30m`, `12h`, `30d`, or `never`.
- Expired objects return `410 EXPIRED` with `Cache-Control: no-store`.
- Expiration only changes public availability; it does not invoke R2 deletion.
- `leenk-static.sh` v1.2.0 supports `upload --expires DURATION`.
- Production Worker version: `43e5351f-a3aa-4960-9961-d1b72e0d8329`
- Application commit: `418e02b`
- Dotfiles/client commit: `a3e2bdf`

Live proof:

- Default upload: `2026-07-17 13:39:20Z` → `2026-07-31 13:39:20Z`.
- One-minute override returned `200` before its deadline and `410` afterward.
- The `410` response proves the Worker still received the stored object and evaluated its expiry metadata.
- The approved probe was subsequently removed; public and remote R2 checks now report it missing.
- Homepage remains `200`.
- [CI](https://github.com/yusoofsh/leenk/actions/runs/29584671423) and [CodeQL](https://github.com/yusoofsh/leenk/actions/runs/29584670351) passed.

Cloudflare lifecycle rules were intentionally avoided because they physically delete expired objects; R2 custom metadata plus Worker enforcement provides logical expiry instead. [R2 lifecycle documentation](https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/buckets/object-lifecycles/), [R2 Workers API](https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/api/workers/workers-api-reference/).

Unrelated `.node-version` and dotfiles `AGENTS.md` changes remain untouched. Goal completed in about 39 minutes of active execution.
